div.head: The Minister Resident in Iraq (Knabenshue) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
2. The Department’s démarche of December 3See telegram No. 56, December 3, 1940, 5 p.m., to the Minister Resident in Iraq, Foreign Relations, 1940, vol. iii, p. 716. and British and Greek victories against the Italians, and the Turkish démarche culminating as reported in my telegram No. 1, January 2, were undoubtedly the deciding factors in averting a serious crisis between
Iraq and Great Britain. The President’s radio address of Sunday lastAddress by President Roosevelt, December 29, 1940, Department of State Bulletin, January 4, 1941, p. 3. has been a further important contribution to the same end. The RegentEmir Abdul Ilah, maternal uncle of the boy King, Faisal II. is reported to be withholding his signature to practically all documents sent to him by the Cabinet.

However, the Prime Minister,Rashid Ali al-Gailani. supported I am told, by the MuftiGrand Mufti of Jerusalem, Haj Amin al-Husseini, a political refugee from Palestine. is still persisting in his efforts to remain in power, while the Minister of DefenseGen. Taha al-Hashimi. who is also acting as Minister of Economics is reported to be endeavoring to defeat British refusal to provide dollars by seeking authority to force Iraqi exporters to turn over their dollar exchange to the Government for dinars in order to provide the Government with the dollars necessary for their purchases of war materials in the United States. This would, of course, adversely affect our normal trade with Iraq.
